Our targeted treatments create a safe barrier around your Bulls Head property, ensuring year-round protection.

The mix of single-family homes with yards and the neighborhood's tree-lined streets require professional tick management to keep outdoor spaces safe for families.

faqs.question: Why is tick control important in Bulls Head?
faqs.answer: Bulls Head's location near wooded areas and parks like Clove Lakes Park creates prime tick habitats. Professional tick control protects your family and pets from Lyme disease, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, and other serious tick-borne illnesses common in Staten Island.

faqs.question: How much does tick control cost in Bulls Head?
faqs.answer: Our tick control services in Bulls Head range from $125 to $325 depending on property size and infestation severity. We provide free inspections and customized treatment plans to fit your specific needs and budget.

faqs.question: When is tick season in Bulls Head, Staten Island?
faqs.answer: Ticks are active in Bulls Head from early spring through late fall, with peak activity from April through September. However, mild winters can extend tick season, making year-round prevention important for Staten Island properties.

faqs.question: Are your tick treatments safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-registered products specifically formulated for residential tick control. Our treatments are safe for children and pets once dried, typically within 2-3 hours, and our technicians follow strict safety protocols during every application.
